detail.rationale: W1-3's plan.yaml entry (authored during the 2026-05-16 bootstrap) lists 5 files in output_contract but its acceptance_criteria + purpose explicitly require wiring MultiHorizonAnticipatoryLearning into ExperimentManager — which requires touching python_refactor/src/experiments/experiment_manager.py. The 5-file list is a planning inconsistency I am closing now via replan-resize. The expanded files list adds experiment_manager.py (1 file). No new acceptance criteria; no schedule change; no dependency change. Resize is the smallest available action — split would create an unnecessary 2-PR cascade for what is a single coherent wiring unit.

detail.operator_note: Approved: scope-resize to add experiment_manager.py per directive #4 (plan.yaml mutation paired with accept in same diff). Authored on chore/replan-W1-3-resize branch to keep feat/w1-3's P3 contract-alone discipline clean.

Wave W1 wave-close ceremony follows W2 merge.

detail.reflection:
### What worked

3 new files, scaffold-only. Production-algorithm code untouched.
  * validation_matrix.py — CLI driver with --scenario / --window /
    --seed / --output / --dry-run. SCENARIOS + WINDOWS dicts encode
    the W6-3 plan directly. Manifest includes git SHA + uv.lock
    SHA + scaffold version for reproducibility receipts.
  * aggregate_validation.py — walks results/ tree, produces summary
    CSV + sibling JSON. Skip-on-non-ok (excludes dry-runs/errors).
  * figures.py — per-run + comparison modes. Headless matplotlib.
    Graceful degrade when expected metric absent (per the W7-1
    contract honest scar).
Dry-run smoke-test PASSES end-to-end: scenario S0 / paper / seed 1
→ manifest.json with status=dry-run-ok + full config dump.

### What broke

Real-run path is unverified by W7-1 — it imports ExperimentManager
but doesn't actually execute. W8's job is to smoke-test the real-run
path against S0 (no learning, simplest case), then expand to S1-S4.
The scaffold's metric-output schema (long format) is what the
aggregator + figure helpers expect; if ExperimentManager's
final_metrics dict doesn't match in W8, schema-bridge work
surfaces then. Honest scar documented in the validation_matrix.py
docstring.

### What you'd change

W8 will validate the real-run path end-to-end. If
ExperimentManager's final_metrics surface doesn't yet emit
wealth_trajectory / lambda_trajectory / belief_trajectory as
named keys, that's an extension unit for W8 to land in the
src/ layer.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: scaffold-only commits with dry-run-only smoke-test are
sufficient evidence of correctness. CHALLENGE: real-run might
surface integration bugs in src/ that the dry-run never exercises.

New carries:
- W7-1-CARRY-1: Real-run path is unverified pending W8 smoke-test.
  Dry-run + scaffold-only commits are the green-light deliverable
  of W7-1.

Scars carried forward:
- W6-2-CARRY-1 (CSV filename parens) unchanged — affects this
  scaffold's `asset_files_glob` for the paper window; W8 may need
  null-delimited handling depending on which loader the existing
  experiments use.

detail.reflection:
### What worked

~350-line doc covers all 7 operator-named analytics categories:
reports (§2 descriptive), statistical tests (§3 inferential),
comparisons (§3.1 pairwise + §3.2 effect size), multi-factor
analyses (§4 ANOVA + synergy contrast + ablation), data
segmentation (§5 year/regime/sub-window), data viz (§6 catalog of
12 figure types), tables (§7 spec of 8 tables A-H). Plus
reproducibility receipts (§8), interpretation playbook (§9),
W8 checklist (§10), anti-goals (§11).

Each statistical test cited to methodology source (paper §V-D for
Mann-Whitney; Holm-Bonferroni for multi-comparison correction).
Each figure linked to a paper figure when applicable (F1↔Fig 4,
F2↔Fig 5, F3↔Fig 4 a/b/c/d).

Interpretation playbook (§9) is the load-bearing section — it
translates raw numbers into actionable findings, with explicit
failure-mode → action mapping. Anti-goals (§11) prevents
scope-creep into Bayesian / causal / cross-baseline territory.

Status framing throughout: this is a PLAN. W8 executes it.

### What broke

None at this unit's level.

### What you'd change

When W8 executes, the §9 interpretation playbook should be tested
empirically — for each row, does the predicted observation actually
occur as theorised? Refine the playbook based on what the data shows.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: the planned 12 figures + 8 tables map cleanly to the
metrics ExperimentManager emits. CHALLENGE: at W8 execution time
some metric channels may be absent or differently-named than the
plan assumes.

No new carries. All prior carries unchanged.

detail.reflection:
### What worked

Template skeleton mirrors ANALYTICS-PLAN.md structure exactly (10
sections, 8 tables A-H, 12 figures F1-F12, paper-comparison Table H,
interpretation playbook walk-through). Every numeric cell is a 🚧
marker — no fabricated values, no plausible-looking-but-fake numbers
that could mislead a future reader who skims the doc.

W8 execution becomes mechanical: produce CSVs, run table generators
+ figure generators, then s/🚧/actual_value/g. The structure is
settled; only cells need filling.

### What broke

None.

### What you'd change

None at this unit's level. W8 should preserve the template's
honesty discipline — every cell must come from a real run with a
real receipt, never a "best guess" interpolation.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: the template's 🚧-marker discipline survives execution.
CHALLENGE: under time pressure, a future agent could fabricate
numbers to make the doc "look complete" — the wave gate must
refuse populated docs whose receipts don't match real runs.

No new carries. All prior carries unchanged.

detail.reflection:
### What worked

Scaffold (validation_matrix.py) is verified callable end-to-end up
to the data-loading layer:
  - Config construction OK
  - ExperimentManager constructor invocation FIXED in W8-1
    (was wrong kwarg `experiment_dir=`; corrected to suite-config dict
    pattern matching run_experiments.py)
  - Data-path glob handling clarified: data_loader takes literal
    paths, not globs. Updated WINDOWS to use FTSE-updated CSV for
    both windows (paper-window data loader is W8-1-CARRY-1).

These are 2 real integration fixes in the scaffold (in-scope).

### What broke

PRE-EXISTING SRC/ BUGS CASCADE.

After fixing the data path, smoke-test surfaced a `KeyError: None`
in pandas `pivot()` deeper in the data_loader / experiment_manager
chain — another pre-existing bug latent in code that was never
exercised end-to-end before W8-1.

Plus the earlier `bool(DataFrame)` ambiguity at
experiment_manager.py:190 surfaced before this one.

My W8-1 scope is validation_matrix.py only. Fixing the src/
integration chain bugs would dwarf the original scope and risk
scope-creep without replan. Per directive #4 (paired plan-mutation
+ replan accept) the correct move is to file the src/ bugs as
carries and let a focused investigation wave (W9 candidate) take
them in proper scope.

The acceptance criterion "smoke-test exits 0 without --dry-run"
is NOT met. The acceptance criterion "manifest.json shows
status=ok" is NOT met. Honest scar — not laundered.

Partial success: the scaffold's reachability is verified — config
builds, ExperimentManager constructs, data_loader is called. The
failure is inside the chain, not in the scaffold.

### What you'd change

W9 should be a focused "data-loader integration fixup" wave that
investigates + fixes the src/ bugs cascading from the smoke-test.
Expected size: M-L (1-3 units). After W9 lands, re-run W8-1's
smoke-test as part of W9's gate to verify the cascade is closed.

Alternative: scope W9 narrowly to "smoke-test passes for S0/paper"
and defer per-scenario fixes to W9+ units.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: pre-existing src/ code is callable end-to-end because
unit tests pass. CHALLENGE: unit tests don't exercise the full
data-loader → experiment_manager → train_loop chain. Dead-code-
coming-alive pattern (6th instance) — bugs lie dormant until
smoke-test fires the full chain.

New carries:
- W8-1-CARRY-1: paper-window data path (98 per-asset CSVs at
  legacy-cpp/executable/data/ftse-original/) needs a custom loader
  extension. The data_loader treats asset_files as literal paths,
  not globs. Smoke-test temporarily uses the FTSE-updated CSV
  for both windows.
- W8-1-CARRY-2: pre-existing src/ integration bugs surfaced by
  end-to-end smoke-test:
    * experiment_manager.py:190 — `bool(DataFrame)` ambiguity
    * data_loader → pandas.pivot() KeyError(None) cascade
    * possibly more deeper in the chain
  None of these were exercised before W8-1's smoke-test attempt.
  Dead-code-coming-alive pattern, 6th instance across the wave
  chain. Investigation + fix needs a dedicated W9 unit.

Scars carried forward:
- W7-1-CARRY-1 is PARTIALLY closed — the scaffold IS callable + reaches
  data-loading. Full end-to-end success blocked on W8-1-CARRY-2's
  src/ fixes.
- W6-2-CARRY-1 (CSV-parens shell-grep) unchanged.

detail.reflection:
### What worked

Pure functions, no I/O, scipy.stats under the hood where available
(mann_whitney_u, wilcoxon, welch_t, one_way_anova) + hand
implementations where scipy doesn't provide directly (cohens_d,
cliffs_delta, bootstrap_ci, holm_bonferroni). 16/16 tests pass
with equation-level assertions against known-analytical cases.

Holm-Bonferroni implementation tested against the textbook example
([0.01, 0.04, 0.03] → [0.03, 0.06, 0.06]) — confirms the step-down
+ monotone-enforce + original-order-restore logic.

Cliff's δ uses numpy broadcasting (`x[:, None] - y[None, :]`) for
O(nm) all-pairs comparison without Python loops.

Each docstring cites methodology source — paper §V-D for Mann-Whitney,
Romano et al. 2006 for Cliff's δ magnitudes.

### What broke

None at this unit's level.

### What you'd change

None.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: scipy.stats wrappers + 4 hand implementations are enough
for the analytics layer. CHALLENGE: when W8-4 runs the synergy
contrast it may need a paired-by-seed bootstrap variant — the
current bootstrap_ci is independent-resample only.

No new carries.

Scars carried forward:
- W8-1-CARRY-2 unchanged (src/ bug cascade — independent of W8-2).

detail.reflection:
### What worked

Pure functions, no I/O — same shape as W8-2. 8 table generators
(A-H) + 3 number-formatting helpers (fmt_sigfig, fmt_pvalue,
fmt_effect) in ~285 lines.

Each generator: (1) returns "_(Table X: no data)_" on empty input,
(2) builds Markdown header + separator, (3) iterates rows applying
formatting helpers, (4) returns the joined string. Same pattern A→H
makes them trivial to read together.

Number formatting per ANALYTICS-PLAN.md §7:
 - fmt_sigfig: digits = sig − floor(log10(|v|)) − 1, NaN-safe, 0-safe.
 - fmt_pvalue: '<0.001' for very small p, else 3 sig figs (so
   p=0.0421 stays readable as "0.0421", p=1e-10 collapses to "<0.001").
 - fmt_effect: 3 decimals fixed (Cohen's d / Cliff's δ scale).

Table F uses an inner grouping pass to widen scenarios × metrics
into one row per (regime, scenario) with mean ± std cells — the
only non-trivial generator. Tested with a 2-scenario × 2-metric
example confirming the header has both metrics and both rows render
with combined cells.

15/15 tests pass on first run after one test-assertion tightening
(see what_didnt).

### What broke

Initial test assertion `assertIn("[0.012, 0.08", out)` for the
Table D CI brackets was too narrow — the 4-sig-fig formatter
pads trailing zeros (0.012 → "0.01200", 0.08 → "0.08000"), so the
bracket substring is "[0.01200, 0.08000]". This is correct per the
ANALYTICS-PLAN §7 spec (4 sig figs default) — the test was wrong,
not the formatter.

Fix: assertion now checks for "0.01200", "0.08000", "[", "]", and
"synergy" separately — robust to formatter precision choices and
still pins the bracket-rendered CI shape.

### What you'd change

None at this unit's level. The trailing-zero padding is a property
of the formula `f"{v:.{digits}f}"` — when |v| < 1 and sig > number
of non-trailing-zero digits, fixed-decimal formatting will pad. If
a future caller wants stripped trailing zeros, add a `strip_trailing=True`
kwarg to fmt_sigfig; not needed for W8-3 / W8-4.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: a test substring matching the "intuitive" rendering of a
number is robust. CHALLENGE: significant-figure formatters pad
trailing zeros when |v| < 1 — the intuitive substring fails for
precisely the values where padding matters. Test against the
formatter's actual output shape, not a hand-rolled expectation.

No new carries.

Scars carried forward:
- W8-1-CARRY-1 (paper-window data loader gap) and W8-1-CARRY-2
  (src/ bool(DataFrame) bug cascade) unchanged — independent of W8-3.
detail.what_worked:
Pure functions, no I/O — same shape as W8-2. 8 table generators
(A-H) + 3 number-formatting helpers (fmt_sigfig, fmt_pvalue,
fmt_effect) in ~285 lines.

Each generator: (1) returns "_(Table X: no data)_" on empty input,
(2) builds Markdown header + separator, (3) iterates rows applying
formatting helpers, (4) returns the joined string. Same pattern A→H
makes them trivial to read together.

Number formatting per ANALYTICS-PLAN.md §7:
 - fmt_sigfig: digits = sig − floor(log10(|v|)) − 1, NaN-safe, 0-safe.
 - fmt_pvalue: '<0.001' for very small p, else 3 sig figs (so
   p=0.0421 stays readable as "0.0421", p=1e-10 collapses to "<0.001").
 - fmt_effect: 3 decimals fixed (Cohen's d / Cliff's δ scale).

Table F uses an inner grouping pass to widen scenarios × metrics
into one row per (regime, scenario) with mean ± std cells — the
only non-trivial generator. Tested with a 2-scenario × 2-metric
example confirming the header has both metrics and both rows render
with combined cells.

15/15 tests pass on first run after one test-assertion tightening
(see what_didnt).
detail.what_broke:
Initial test assertion `assertIn("[0.012, 0.08", out)` for the
Table D CI brackets was too narrow — the 4-sig-fig formatter
pads trailing zeros (0.012 → "0.01200", 0.08 → "0.08000"), so the
bracket substring is "[0.01200, 0.08000]". This is correct per the
ANALYTICS-PLAN §7 spec (4 sig figs default) — the test was wrong,
not the formatter.

Fix: assertion now checks for "0.01200", "0.08000", "[", "]", and
"synergy" separately — robust to formatter precision choices and
still pins the bracket-rendered CI shape.
detail.what_youd_change:
None at this unit's level. The trailing-zero padding is a property
of the formula `f"{v:.{digits}f}"` — when |v| < 1 and sig > number
of non-trailing-zero digits, fixed-decimal formatting will pad. If
a future caller wants stripped trailing zeros, add a `strip_trailing=True`
kwarg to fmt_sigfig; not needed for W8-3 / W8-4.
detail.assumption_to_challenge:
Assumed: a test substring matching the "intuitive" rendering of a
number is robust. CHALLENGE: significant-figure formatters pad
trailing zeros when |v| < 1 — the intuitive substring fails for
precisely the values where padding matters. Test against the
formatter's actual output shape, not a hand-rolled expectation.

No new carries.

Scars carried forward:
- W8-1-CARRY-1 (paper-window data loader gap) and W8-1-CARRY-2
  (src/ bool(DataFrame) bug cascade) unchanged — independent of W8-3.

detail.reflection:
### What worked

Pure-orchestration layer composes W8-2 stats + W8-3 tables into a
populated VALIDATION-RESULTS.md. ~330 lines of code, 9/9 tests pass,
0.9s for the full W8 analytics test suite (40 tests across stats +
tables + report).

Substitution strategy: regex-locate each Table A-H section header,
find the first Markdown table block under it, replace contiguous
pipe-prefixed lines with the generated string. Sections without
data keep their 🚧 placeholders → graceful incompleteness, which
is what the contract demanded.

Builders convert raw summary CSV + per-run buckets into the
row-dicts that the W8-3 generators expect. build_table_b_rows
runs Mann-Whitney + Cohen's d + Cliff's δ + CLES over all scenario
pairs, then Holm-Bonferroni across the family at the end (matches
ANALYTICS-PLAN.md §3.1 family-wise control).

build_table_d_rows implements the synergy contrast Δ_interaction
= (S2 − S0) − 2*(S1 − S0) = S2 − 2*S1 + S0, with 10k-bootstrap
CI. Verified with a clean signal: S0=1.00, S1=1.05, S2=1.20 →
Δ=0.10, CI positive → "synergy".

Receipt block per ANALYTICS-PLAN §8: git SHA (8 char), uv.lock
sha256 fingerprint (16 char), ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, n_runs from
summary, seed list (collapsed to [first, ..., last] (N seeds)
when > 4), reproducibility command echoed verbatim.

CLI roundtrip test writes a synthetic summary + 2-run results tree
+ template to a tempdir, runs main(), reads back the output, and
confirms the receipt populates and Table A renders.

### What broke

Tables C/E/F/G/H not wired: ANOVA over Multi-Horizon levels
(C), per-horizon ablation (E), regime segmentation (F), sub-window
stability (G), and paper-comparison (H) all need per-run inputs
the W7-1 scaffold doesn't yet emit (regime tags, sub-window splits,
paper-Table-I values). The generators are ready (W8-3); the
builders are stubs that return [] → the template keeps its 🚧
markers for those sections. Filed as W8-CARRY-3 (analytics-builder
completion) — not blocking wave-close because the orchestrator's
contract is "graceful incompleteness."

Bootstrap CI in build_table_d_rows uses independent resampling
per scenario, not paired-by-seed. If seeds align across scenarios
(which W7-1 does enforce), a paired bootstrap would be tighter.
Filed as part of W8-CARRY-3.

### What you'd change

None at this unit's level. The orchestrator's contract is to wire
what's wireable and leave the rest gracefully unfilled. C/E/F/G/H
are W8-CARRY-3 deliverables for the next wave.

### Assumption to challenge

Assumed: the analytics layer can ship complete even though the data
pipeline upstream of it is broken. CHALLENGE: shipping a "complete"
analytics layer against a broken pipeline means we ship code paths
(C/E/F/G/H builders) that have never been exercised against real
data — a 7th instance of the dead-code-coming-alive pattern in
waiting. W9 must close the cascade so W8-CARRY-3 can be honestly
exercised.

New carries:
- W8-CARRY-3: complete builders for Tables C, E, F, G, H once
  W7-1 emits the inputs they need (per-horizon ablation, regime
  tags, sub-window splits, paper-Table-I gold values). Includes
  paired-by-seed bootstrap upgrade for Table D.

Scars carried forward:
- W8-1-CARRY-1 (paper-window data loader) and W8-1-CARRY-2 (src/
  bool(DataFrame) cascade) unchanged — independent of W8-4.
